rescue_reasoning: The verbatim text explicitly targets geothermal heating potential: 'survey for geothermal heating potential' / 'areas suitable for geo-energy will be surveyed'. Geothermal heating enables low-carbon heat deployment and thus mitigation, even if no explicit 'emissions reduction' token is present. Climate screen verdict is 'explicit_self', so the row should remain in the climate dataset under Mitigation.
policy_statement: Survey areas suitable for geo-energy (geothermal heating potential survey).
mechanism_description: The policy will survey areas suitable for geo-energy to identify geothermal heating potential, supporting future geothermal heating deployment and associated emissions reductions from replacing fossil heating.
primary_causal_pathway: Geothermal resource assessment → enables geothermal heating deployment → fossil heating displacement → ↓ fuel combustion → ↓ GHG emissions → ↓ atmospheric forcing
causal_mechanism_detail:
1) Change in human activity/system: Public agencies/utilities conduct geothermal resource mapping/surveys (e.g., geophysical surveys, test wells, heat-flow mapping) and publish results to guide project siting and investment.  
2) Mediating physical process: Verified subsurface temperature/flow characteristics reduce exploration uncertainty and transaction costs, accelerating deployment of geothermal wells/heat exchangers and integration into building heating or district heating networks.  
3) Climate-relevant outcome: Delivered heat shifts from fossil boilers/CHP (gas, oil, coal, peat) to geothermal heat, reducing lifecycle CO₂e (primarily operational CO₂) per unit of heat supplied.
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is enabling geothermal heat to replace fossil-based heating, thereby reducing direct fuel combustion GHG emissions.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: The assessment is an enabling step whose main downstream effect is fossil-heat substitution with geothermal, yielding net GHG reductions.  
2) Why this primary over alternatives: While assessments can improve planning efficiency (a resource-efficiency flavor) and may have minor resilience co-benefits (diversifying heat supply), the principal climate-relevant consequence of adopting this mechanism is emissions mitigation via fuel-switching.  
3) Edge cases applied: If geothermal were primarily used to increase total heat supply without displacing fossil heat, or if the grid/heat system is already near-zero carbon, mitigation impact could be smaller; nevertheless the mechanism’s intended and typical dominant pathway remains fossil displacement.  
4) Applies to ALL instances sharing the mechanism: Any geothermal resource assessment aimed at enabling geothermal heating deployment is fundamentally a pre-deployment mitigation enabler through decarbonizing heat supply.  
5) Secondary subtype assignment deferred: Any further labeling (e.g., “data/planning instrument,” “district heating transition,” “renewable heat”) depends on policy-specific details and is deferred to policy-level evidence.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: If geothermal development induces additional energy use (rebound) or materially increases electricity demand for heat pumps supplied by high-carbon electricity, net GHG benefits could be reduced; classification remains Mitigation because the dominant intended pathway is fossil heating displacement.
key_indicators: "areas suitable for geo-energy will be surveyed"; "survey for geothermal heating potential"
co_benefits: Energy security; local air quality improvements; reduced heating cost volatility; local employment in surveying/drilling services
instance_edge_case_notes: Could be interpreted as information generation/publication (information-disclosure), but the text frames it as a governmental survey/planning activity rather than a disclosure requirement on private actors; classified as planning-strategy.
